title: Investigations & CAPAs (01–15)
normalized_content:
phase_0_5b_investigations_capas_01_to_15.md
Each package will include:
● Investigation report summary (problem, timeline, root cause, impact).
● Linked deviation/complaint/audit finding.
● CAPA details (actions, owners, due dates, effectiveness checks).
● Links to batches, equipment, suppliers, and SOPs.
Phase 0.5B – Detailed Investigation & CAPA
Packages (01–15)
Organization: Aetheris BioPharma Private Limited
Scope: Detailed investigation reports and CAPA records for major historical quality events
Date: 2026-07-11
Version: R0
All records below follow Aetheris numbering conventions and are consistent with Phases
0.1–0.4, Master Index 0.5A, Core SOPs 0.5B, and Batch Records 0.5B.
Investigation 01
Document ID: VISK-QA-RPT-2025-0038-R0
Title: Investigation into Visible Cosmetic Particulate Complaint for Injectra 1 g
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: VISK
Effective Date: 2025-07-05
Related Complaint: VISK-CMP-2025-0026
Related Deviation: VISK-DEV-2025-0094
Related CAPA: VISK-CAPA-2025-0028
Related Batch: V20012506002 (VISK-BMR-FPP2001-2506-002)
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-001, EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOPs:
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0303-R0 (Cleaning/Disinfection)

● VISK-QC-SOP-QC-0510-R0 (EM)
1. Problem Statement
A UAE distributor reported a visible cosmetic particulate in one vial of Injectra 1 g, batch
V20012506002. The product was not administered. The particulate appeared as a faint white
speck on the inner vial wall near the stopper.
2. Timeline
● 2025-06-15 to 2025-06-17: Batch manufactured.
● 2025-06-18: Complaint received (VISK-CMP-2025-0026).
● 2025-06-19: Deviation raised (VISK-DEV-2025-0094) due to concurrent Grade B viable
air alert during manufacturing.
● 2025-06-20 to 2025-07-02: Investigation executed.
● 2025-07-05: Investigation report issued.
3. Investigation Summary
Lab Examination:
● Returned vial examined under controlled lighting.
● Particulate confirmed as external cosmetic speck; no evidence of product interaction.
● Sterility test on sibling vials: negative.
Manufacturing Review:
● EM data showed one Grade B viable air count at 4 cfu/m3 (alert level, not action).
● Cleaning logs for sterile block showed one deviation in mop rotation sequence, corrected
same day.

Root Cause Analysis (5 Whys + Fishbone):
● Likely multi-factor:
○ Minor cleaning sequence deviation.
○ Transient EM alert indicating possible airborne contribution.
○ No evidence of systemic aseptic failure.
Impact Assessment:
● Isolated cosmetic issue; no confirmed sterility or safety impact.
● One vial affected out of ~10,000 produced.
4. CAPA: VISK-CAPA-2025-0028
Title: Visible Particulate Complaint and Wash/Fill Control Enhancement
Actions:
1.Enhanced visual inspection criteria and training for filling line operators.
2. Increased EM trend review frequency (monthly to biweekly for 6 months).
3. Reinforced cleaning sequence compliance with QA spot checks.
Owner: Meera Iyer
Due Dates:
● Training: 2025-07-20 – Completed.
● EM trend review: 2025-07 to 2026-01 – Completed.
● Cleaning compliance checks: 2025-08 to 2026-02 – Completed.
Effectiveness Check:
● Metric: No repeat similar complaints for next 6 Injectra batches.
● Result: No recurrence; CAPA closed 2026-03.

5. Linked Records
● Batch: VISK-BMR-FPP2001-2506-002
● Complaint: VISK-CMP-2025-0026
● Deviation: VISK-DEV-2025-0094
● CAPA: VISK-CAPA-2025-0028
● PQR: VISK-PQR-FPP2001-2025-001
Investigation 02
Document ID: PASH-QA-RPT-2025-0041-R0
Title: Compression Force Drift During Aethcard 10 mg Batch
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: PASH
Effective Date: 2025-04-10
Related Complaint: PASH-CMP-2025-0048
Related Deviation: PASH-DEV-2025-0142
Related CAPA: PASH-CAPA-2025-0037
Related Batch: P10012503001 (PASH-BMR-FPP1001-2503-001)
Related Product: FPP-1001
Related Equipment: EQ-OSD-01-002
Related SOPs:
● PASH-OSD-SOP-OSD-0205-R0
1. Problem Statement
During manufacture of Aethcard 10 mg batch P10012503001, hourly compression force trend
showed drift towards upper limit. One post-market complaint of tablet breakage was received
for this batch.

2. Timeline
● 2025-03-10 to 2025-03-12: Batch manufactured.
● 2025-04-02: Complaint received (PASH-CMP-2025-0048).
● 2025-04-05: Deviation raised (PASH-DEV-2025-0142).
● 2025-04-06 to 2025-04-09: Investigation.
● 2025-04-10: Report issued.
3. Investigation Summary
Data Review:
● Compression force trend showed gradual increase from 8.2 kp to 8.9 kp over 6 hours.
● Hardness remained within spec (8.0–9.0 kp), but closer to upper limit.
Equipment Check:
● Punches showed moderate wear; within life but approaching replacement threshold.
● No mechanical fault found.
Root Cause:
● Combined effect of punch wear and insufficiently tight trend escalation limits.
Impact:
● One breakage complaint; no safety issue confirmed.
● Batch within specification.
4. CAPA: PASH-CAPA-2025-0037

Title: Compression Parameter and Blister Robustness Improvement
Actions:
1. Tighten compression force internal alert limits.
2. Advance punch replacement schedule.
3. Enhance blister packaging robustness check.
Owner: D. Anitha
Due Dates:
● Limit change: 2025-04-20 – Completed.
● Punch schedule: 2025-05-01 – Completed.
● Blister check: 2025-05-10 – Completed.
Effectiveness:
● No repeat compression-drift complaints in next 5 Aethcard batches.
● CAPA closed 2025-08.
5. Linked Records
● Batch: PASH-BMR-FPP1001-2503-001
● Complaint: PASH-CMP-2025-0048
● Deviation: PASH-DEV-2025-0142
● CAPA: PASH-CAPA-2025-0037
● PQR: PASH-PQR-FPP1001-2025-001
Investigation 03
Document ID: PASH-QA-RPT-2025-0062-R0
Title: Real-Time Documentation Failure in API Charging Process
Owner: Meera Iyer

Site: PASH
Effective Date: 2025-07-01
Related Deviation: PASH-DEV-2025-0221
Related CAPA: PASH-CAPA-2025-0049
Related Batch: API-1012604001 (PASH-BMR-API101-2604-001)
Related Product: API-101
Related Equipment: EQ-API-01-001
Related SOPs:
● PASH-API-SOP-API-0302-R0
1. Problem Statement
During API-101 manufacture, reactor charging steps were not documented in real time;
entries made 2 hours post-activity.
2. Investigation Summary
Root Cause:
● Operator workload and ambiguous expectation on “contemporaneous” recording.
Impact:
● No product-quality impact confirmed; data accurate but not contemporaneous.
3. CAPA: PASH-CAPA-2025-0049
Title: Real-Time Documentation Control in API Charging
Actions:

1. Refresher training on GDP and contemporaneous recording.
2. Batch-record sampling across four campaigns.
3. Supervisor spot checks during charging.
Owner: K. Ramesh
Effectiveness:
● No repeat findings in next 6 months.
● CAPA closed 2025-12.
Investigation 04
Document ID: VISK-QA-RPT-2025-0054-R0
Title: WFI Conductivity Transient Above Internal Alert
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: VISK
Effective Date: 2025-08-10
Related Deviation: VISK-DEV-2025-0156
Related CAPA: VISK-CAPA-2025-0035
Related Product: FPP-2001/2002/2003
Related Equipment: EQ-UTL-002
Related SOPs:
● VISK-ENG-SOP-ENG-0605-R0
1. Problem Statement
WFI conductivity briefly exceeded internal alert (1.4 µS/cm; spec ≤ 1.5) for 8 minutes.
2. Summary
● Cause: Transient resin bed channeling.
● No batches impacted; water not used during excursion window.

3. CAPA: VISK-CAPA-2025-0035
● Enhanced sanitization frequency.
● 30-day utility trend review.
● No recurrence; CAPA closed 2025-11.
Investigation 05
Document ID: CORP-QA-RPT-2025-0017-R0
Title: QMS Closure Rationale Without Contemporaneous Comment
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2025-08-20
Related Deviation: CORP-DEV-2025-0031
Related CAPA: CORP-CAPA-2025-0008
Related System: Enterprise QMS
1. Problem Statement
Some CAPAs closed with minimal rationale entered days after actual closure decision.
2. Root Cause
● Workflow allowed closure before rationale entry; no hard stop.
3. CAPA: CORP-CAPA-2025-0008

● System update to enforce rationale before closure.
● Audit-trail review post-update.
● No recurrence; CAPA closed 2025-12.

Investigation 15
Document ID: VISK-QA-RPT-2026-0048-R0
Title: Irregular Crimp Appearance and Possible Stopper Residue for Injectra 1 g
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: VISK
Effective Date: Open (2026-07-11)

Related Deviation: VISK-DEV-2026-0114
Related Complaint: VISK-CMP-2026-0034
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOPs:
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0309-R0
Related Employees:
● EMP-0303 (M. Deepak)
● EMP-0417 (R. Chandra)
● EMP-0418 (S. Fernandes)
● EMP-0204 (T. Srinivas)
● EMP-0401 (J. Jose)
Related Suppliers: SUP-003, SUP-008
1. Problem Statement
Post-fill review identified irregular crimp appearance and faint whitish residue near stopper
edge on 2–3 vials. Later, distributor reported loose flip-off caps and powder-like ring near
stopper.
2. Current Status
● Investigation open.
● Batch on QA hold.
● No CAPA initiated yet.
● Evidence package and follow-up missing-evidence pack created in Phase 0.4/0.5.
